pages.text_excerpt: Campaigns | Caroline Dinenage MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home News In Parliament Local Campaigns Newsletters Contact Campaigns Expanding Health Provision Across the Constituency Caroline has been a regular campaigner for health and social care provision since her election in 2010. This passion was recognised when she was appointed as... Improving Links Across Portsmouth Harbour Caroline has a deep appreciation for the historical link which Gosport shares with Portsmouth. Connectivity across the harbour has played a vital role in... Monitoring Haslar Immigration Removal Centre The Home Office is still in the process of redeveloping the Haslar IRC site - which was delayed due to a number of factors including asbestos. When it reopens... Cleaning Up Our Seas and Waterways Caroline regularly meets with Southern Water and keeps local people updated on what she is doing to ensure that the water infrastructure is appropriate. In... Regenerating Fort Blockhouse Caroline has emphasised the potential of Fort Blockhouse to make a significant economic contribution to Gosport in the House of Commons Chamber on many... Supporting Local Community Organisations Caroline believes that voluntary organisations are the backbone of the Gosport Constituency’s community. She has visited various local and national charities... h2: What is RSS?
h3: Caroline Dinenage MP Conservative MP for Gosport
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: About RSS | Caroline Dinenage MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home News In Parliament Local Campaigns Newsletters Contact About RSS RSS feeds and news syndication News feeds Events feeds You can read the latest news from this website using your news reader, or display them on your own website using RSS (Rich Site Summary) XML documents, wherever you see the orange RSS symbol. What is RSS? RSS is a defacto XML standard invented by Netscape for news distribution, now used by thousands of sites. RSS is an open format that uses a simple set of XML tags to define a news "channel" that allows any site to provide basic meta-information about your new content (headline, link, description). Whenever you use our RSS feeds publicly (where anyone but yourself would be reading them), you should provide proper attribution to this website. We cannot provide advice or technical support in the coding and integration of our RSS feeds into your site or software. Additionally we ask that you cache the RSS feed and do not query more than once in any hour. Caroline Dinenage MP Conservative MP for Gosport Footer About RSS Accessibility Cookies Privacy About Caroline Dinenage Promoted by Glenn Duggan on behalf of Caroline Dinenage, both at 167 Stoke Road, Gosport, PO12 1SE Copyright 2026 Caroline Dinenage MP.
